unit.name: From static embeddings to contextual pretraining
unit.description: Motivates whole-model pretraining by contrasting static word embeddings with contextual representations and by showing what can be learned from reconstructing masked text.
unit.summary: This segment reframes the course’s earlier distributional hypothesis in contextual terms: word2vec gives one vector for a surface form like "record," so noun and verb senses are blended into a single embedding, whereas contextual models can represent the token differently depending on surrounding words [ts=903s] [ts=956s]. The instructor then contrasts the old setup—pretrained word embeddings plus a randomly initialized task model—with modern pretraining, where the entire network is trained before downstream fine-tuning [ts=1039s] [ts=1065s]. Pretraining is presented as reconstructing corrupted input: mask part of a naturally occurring sentence and force the model to predict the missing content [ts=1207s] [ts=1246s]. Through examples, the lecture argues that this objective can teach syntax (what determiners fit), co-reference ("her" matching "the woman"), lexical semantics (which sea creatures fit a list), sentiment (predicting whether a movie was bad), and even simple world or reasoning structure such as physical location and numerical continuation [ts=1277s] [ts=1446s].

key_points.text: Static embeddings collapse multiple senses of a word like "record" into one representation.
Whole-model pretraining means pretraining not only the input embeddings but also the contextual network on top of them.
A core self-supervised recipe is to mask part of real text and train the network to reconstruct the missing portion.
Masked prediction can teach syntax by constraining which words can legally fit a context.
Masked prediction can also surface sentiment and simple world knowledge from ordinary text.

kp_catalog.name: Contextual representations and embeddings
kp_catalog.description: A single embedding per word string cannot fully disambiguate sense by context, though it can average across senses in a useful way. Representations whose meaning depends on the surrounding sequence rather than assigning one static vector to each surface form.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because contextual representations and embeddings formalizes how a single embedding per word string cannot fully disambiguate sense by context, though it can average across senses in a useful way, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context while roles split between gateway and supporting and importance labels split between critical and medium.

kp_catalog.name: Pretraining, fine-tuning, and transfer learning
kp_catalog.description: Two-stage learning in which a model first learns generic language structure from large unlabeled corpora and is then adapted to a supervised downstream task. Reusing pretrained CNN features or pretrained weights to solve a new task more effectively when target data is limited.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because pretraining, fine-tuning, and transfer learning formalizes how two-stage learning in which a model first learns generic language structure from large unlabeled corpora and is then adapted to a supervised downstream task, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context though roles split between capstone and gateway.
